response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a seasoned full-stack and systems developer with a broad background across web, mobile, embedded, and developer tooling — but his technical stack is fundamentally misaligned with this role. The position demands deep Java and Kotlin expertise, JVM internals knowledge, and hands-on Spring/enterprise framework experience, none of which are meaningfully present in this resume. Java appears in his skills list and incidentally at Altom (robotic arms, mobile orchestration), but there is no depth, no Kotlin, no Spring/Quarkus/Hibernate, and no JVM-level work. His closest IDE-adjacent experience is writing C/C++ components for NI LabWindows over a decade ago, which is too distant in both technology and time to count as relevant IDE tooling background.
strengths: Genuine and sustained interest in developer tooling — MCP frameworks, test runners, CI/CD infrastructure, and NI LabWindows IDE components show a recurring pattern
Broad polyglot systems experience across web, mobile, embedded, and GIS domains, indicating adaptability and engineering range
Active open-source contributor and workshop facilitator, demonstrating strong communication and knowledge-sharing skills
concerns: No demonstrated Java or Kotlin depth — primary stack is TypeScript/Node.js with only peripheral Java usage in non-JVM contexts
Zero experience with Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or any enterprise JVM framework explicitly required by the role
No JVM-based IDE internals or language tooling experience — NI LabWindows work is C-based, decade-old, and does not translate to the JetBrains/IntelliJ plugin or JVM tooling domain
